 {"id":"bd-1kx7","title":"E2E: extend CI script to run full scenario suite","description":"Extend the E2E runner script to run the full demo_showcase scenario suite (not just smoke), with great logging and CI-friendly controls.

Why:
- The demo_showcase is a huge integration surface; regressions will happen.
- A single, well-instrumented E2E entrypoint makes it easy to validate “everything still works” locally and in CI.

Requirements:
- Profiles:
  - `smoke` (fast): minimal navigation, no panics, basic rendering assertions.
  - `full` (slower): runs every scenario and validates key outputs/artifacts.
  - optional `nightly` (extra slow): stress/resizes, multiple seeds, async-enabled run.
- Scenario selection:
  - allow selecting a subset (e.g., `--scenario docs` or `--scenario wizard,logs`).
- Determinism:
  - accepts a seed (or chooses and prints one) and writes it into the artifact directory.
- Observability:
  - structured JSONL log output + per-step timing
  - on failure, print a clear summary including artifact path and last N steps
- Exit codes:
  - non-zero on failure; zero on success.

Acceptance:
- CI can choose which profile to run (PR vs nightly) without editing the script.
- Failures are actionable: the artifact directory contains enough info to reproduce.

Testing:
- The script itself is validated by running at least the smoke suite in CI.
- Full suite runs either on demand or on nightly schedule.

Notes:
- Keep this script the canonical entrypoint; avoid multiple half-overlapping scripts.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:27.825203081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:36:58.774535400Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:36:58.774396341Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","script","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-1g80","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3449","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3ecr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3nrc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3spt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-6wkl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-miqo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1m7x","title":"E2E scenario: ssh mode smoke (optional/manual)","description":"Optional E2E scenario for wish integration.

Constraints:
- This may be too heavy for default CI.

Plan:
- Generate/use a temp host key.
- Start `demo_showcase ssh` on localhost with an ephemeral port.
- Connect with an SSH client (or a minimal russh client) and ensure:
  - the server accepts a session
  - the UI renders at least one frame
  - a quit sequence cleanly closes the session

Logging:
- Capture server logs + client transcript to artifacts.

Acceptance:
- Provides high confidence that wish integration stays alive over time.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:31.040331959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T02:08:15.973685320Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T02:08:15.973617434Z","close_reason":"Created comprehensive SSH E2E smoke test suite (tests/ssh_e2e.rs) with: server startup test, UI rendering test (requires sshpass), clean disconnect test, bad password rejection test, and comprehensive smoke test. Tests are marked #[ignore] for CI compatibility and run with --ignored flag. Tests generate temp host keys automatically and use ephemeral ports.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","ssh","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1m7x","depends_on_id":"bd-32ba","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m7x","depends_on_id":"bd-3xs0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m7x","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1nwu","title":"Settings: toggles (mouse, animations, no-color, syntax)","description":"Expose runtime toggles:
- mouse on/off
- animations on/off
- force ASCII/no color
- syntax highlighting + line numbers (if enabled)

Implementation notes:
- This page should mutate the live app state, but also reflect the initial defaults from `demo_showcase::Config`.

Acceptance:
- Toggling updates app behavior live without restart.
- Toggles are discoverable and have immediate, visible effects (not hidden flags).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:01.250474646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T16:56:54.222802995Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T16:56:54.222719740Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","settings"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-274o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-2szb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-3s4n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1nzv","title":"Fix example run instructions to use charmed-* package names (no -p bubbletea/glamour staleness)","description":"Several examples still tell users to run `cargo run -p bubbletea` / `-p glamour` / `-p bubbletea-macros`, which is wrong in this workspace because packages are published as `charmed-*` and the workspace package names are `charmed-bubbletea`, `charmed-glamour`, `charmed-bubbletea-macros`, etc. Update comments and any printed help strings.

Targets found via rg:
- crates/bubbletea/examples/counter.rs
- crates/bubbletea/examples/async_fetch.rs (also the fallback eprintln)
- crates/bubbletea-macros/examples/{counter_derive,state_tracking,generic_model}.rs
- crates/glamour/examples/{theme_gallery,syntax_highlighting}.rs (and the theme_gallery printed string)

After edits: cargo fmt/check/clippy + focused cargo check for examples.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T15:57:33.718544722Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T16:00:24.816551136Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T16:00:24.816485564Z","close_reason":"Updated example run instructions and printed hints to use correct charmed-* package names; fmt/check/clippy green","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","examples"]}
 {"id":"bd-1o6y","title":"Implement huh Multiselect, Text, FilePicker, Note field types","description":"Implement missing Huh field types: Multiselect, FilePicker, and Note. (Textarea is tracked separately under bd-212m.4.1.)

Scope:
- Add field structs + state for multiselect, filepicker, note in crates/huh/src/lib.rs.
- Implement update/view logic and keybindings consistent with Go.
- Add validation hooks and theming support for each field.
- Expand conformance fixtures and add unit tests for each field type.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Conformance fixtures for multiselect, text, filepicker, and note pass with no skips.
- Unit tests cover input handling, selection toggles, and rendering for each field.
- FEATURE_PARITY.md updated to reflect field coverage.","notes":"Textarea scope moved to bd-212m.4.1 to keep parity gap tracking focused.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:22.524453153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:33:37.237925265Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T01:19:31.436260994Z","close_reason":"Implemented Text and FilePicker fields. MultiSelect and Note were already present. All field types have builder pattern, validation, keyboard handling, theme support, and view rendering.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1oe0","title":"demo_showcase core: optionally use bubbletea derive macro for one model","description":"Optional showcase: use `bubbletea-macros` derive for *one* small model/component inside demo_showcase.

Why:
- It demonstrates that the workspace’s proc-macro ergonomics actually work in a real app.
- It also gives users a concrete reference for when the derive is worth using.

Scope:
- Pick a small, isolated model (examples):
  - a tiny counter widget in Settings/About
  - a modal state machine (open/close) with a few fields
- Do NOT macro-derive the entire App (keep core architecture explicit and readable).

Acceptance:
- The derived model compiles cleanly and behaves correctly.
- The code includes a brief doc comment explaining why derive was used here.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:52.572059183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:13:24.995657956Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:13:24.995586343Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added InteractionCounter widget with #[derive(Model)] macro demonstration. All 13 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","macros"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1oe0","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1oe0","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1p61","title":"Unit tests: domain action API correctness","description":"Add comprehensive unit tests for the domain action API.

Coverage:
- start job -> job exists + correct initial state
- progress ticks update job correctly
- cancel job -> state transitions + logs/notifications
- retry failed job -> new attempt semantics
- deploy action -> deployment + job correlation

Logging:
- Tests should log action inputs + resulting state diffs for easy debugging.

Acceptance:
- The action API is covered with high confidence and runs fast.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:05.168723242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:24:21.174597921Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:24:21.174533320Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1p61","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1p61","depends_on_id":"bd-3pm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
